name: cf-sys-debug

criteria.name: root_cause_found
criteria.description: The actual root cause of the bug is identified, not just a symptom

scoring.0: Root cause not found — debugging went in the wrong direction entirely
scoring.1: A contributing factor is found but the actual root cause is missed
scoring.2: Root cause identified but explanation lacks precision or supporting evidence
scoring.3: Root cause precisely identified with clear evidence (stack trace, reproduction, code reference)

criteria.name: systematic_approach
criteria.description: Debugging follows the 4-phase methodology: observe, hypothesize, test, fix

scoring.0: Random trial-and-error with no systematic approach
scoring.1: Some structure but phases are mixed or skipped (e.g., jumps to fix without hypothesis)
scoring.2: Follows the 4-phase approach but one phase is superficial
scoring.3: Clear 4-phase progression: observation documented, hypothesis stated, tests run, fix applied

criteria.name: fix_correctness
criteria.description: The applied fix resolves the bug completely

scoring.0: Fix does not resolve the bug
scoring.1: Fix partially resolves the bug but it can still be triggered in some cases
scoring.2: Fix resolves the bug but approach is fragile or has side effects
scoring.3: Fix cleanly resolves the bug with no side effects and handles edge cases

criteria.name: documented
criteria.description: Bug investigation and resolution are documented for future reference

scoring.0: No documentation of the debugging process or findings
scoring.1: Brief mention of the fix but no documentation of the investigation
scoring.2: Investigation documented but missing reproduction steps or prevention advice
scoring.3: Full documentation: symptom, root cause, fix, reproduction steps, and how to prevent recurrence

notes: The 4 phases should be visible in the output even if not explicitly labeled. Complex bugs may require multiple hypothesis-test cycles — this is expected and should not be penalized.
